justification: All pass criteria met: PM/Legal contradiction explicitly identified and escalated with detailed options and recommendation. Clock-start ambiguity identified in low_confidence_areas ('Whether the 2-hour window starts at order placement or at pickup'). Wrong-address exception from Finance noted as BR-6. Auto-refund vs. 3-day processing conflict identified as a formal escalation with three resolution options. 9 business rules extracted with correct invariant classifications. 2 formal escalations plus a rejection reason. Confidence level is 40, well under the 50 threshold.

observations.text: PM/Legal guarantee vs. best-effort contradiction is the centerpiece of the analysis, with a dedicated escalation, three resolution options, and a concrete hybrid recommendation.
Second escalation cleanly captures the three-way conflict between automatic refunds (CS), 3-business-day processing (Finance), and discretionary goodwill (Legal).
Clock-start ambiguity surfaced in both low_confidence_areas and assumptions, with the Ops Lead's concern about pickup delays eating into the window noted.
Wrong-address refund exception extracted as BR-6 with a testable assertion.

9 well-structured business rules with thoughtful invariant classifications — notably BR-1 (10km radius) and BR-3 (15-min pickup) marked as invariants while BR-4 (driver rating) marked as non-invariant with sound justification.
Confidence at 40 with clear reasoning about why the contradiction blocks confident implementation.
Rejection reasons section explicitly states implementation cannot proceed until the core contradiction is resolved — Bird refuses to produce a clean model over an incoherent spec.
Edge cases are thorough and practical — the driver-cancels-at-minute-9-but-delivery-succeeds scenario is particularly sharp.
Business impact analysis identifies the auto-refund exploitation risk and driver compensation gap, which were not in the rubric but demonstrate deep domain reasoning.

Clock-start ambiguity is not surfaced as a formal escalation — it appears in low_confidence_areas and assumptions but lacks the explicit 'ESCALATION: Clock start time is undefined' format the rubric specified.
The ubiquitous language section defines 'Delivery Window' as starting 'from order placement' without flagging this is an assumption — could mislead readers who skip to that section.
